columns.Name: INSTANCE_ID
columns.Comments: Instance Id

columns.Name: SECTION_ID
columns.Comments: Section ID

columns.Name: DEFAULT_FLAG
columns.Comments: DEFAULT_FLAG

columns.Name: DISPLAY_ORDER
columns.Comments: Display Order

columns.Name: SECTION_LAYOUT
columns.Comments: Stores layout option for FUSE UI.

columns.Name: BUSINESS_GROUP_ID
columns.Comments: Identifier of Enterprise, used for multi-tenancy partitioning. Foreign key to HR_ORGANIZATION_UNITS.

columns.Name: PROFILE_TYPE_ID
columns.Comments: Profile Type ID

columns.Name: CONTENT_TYPE_ID
columns.Comments: Content Type ID

columns.Name: PARENT_SECTION_ID
columns.Comments: Parent Section ID

columns.Name: CONTENT_TYPE_RELATION_ID
columns.Comments: Content Type Relation ID

columns.Name: SECTION_ITEM_REQD_FLAG
columns.Comments: Section Item Required Flag

columns.Name: APPROVAL_REQUIRED_FLAG
columns.Comments: Approval Required Flag

columns.Name: QUALIFIER_SET_ID1
columns.Comments: Qualifier Set ID1

columns.Name: QUALIFIER_SET_ID2
columns.Comments: Qualifier Set ID2

columns.Name: SECTION_CONTEXT
columns.Comments: Section Context

columns.Name: SECURITY_CONTEXT
columns.Comments: This column will have the same value as Section_Context for newly created sections. For sections that are upgraded from the old architecture, this column will have the value of Context_Name for the Content Type of the section.

columns.Name: TEMPLATE_FLAG
columns.Comments: Template flag to indicate whether it is template or not

columns.Name: DFF_CONTEXT_NAME
columns.Comments: DFF context name for each section

columns.Name: ANALYTIC_PRIORITY
columns.Comments: Analytic priority to indicate the ordering of the data

columns.Name: ANALYTIC_PRIORITY_SOURCE_CODE
columns.Comments: Analytic priority based on consumer

columns.Name: ACTIVE_FLAG
columns.Comments: Active flag to indicate whether the section is active ('Y') or inactive (null/ 'N')

columns.Name: MODULE_ID
columns.Comments: Seed Data Framework: indicates the module that owns the row. A module is an entry in Application Taxonomy such as a Logical Business Area. When the MODULE_ID column exists and the owner of the row is not specified, then the Seed Data Framework will not extract the row as seed data.

columns.Name: OBJECT_VERSION_NUMBER
columns.Comments: Used to implement optimistic locking. This number is incremented every time that the row is updated. The number is compared at the start and end of a transaction to detect whether another session has updated the row since it was queried.

columns.Name: CREATED_BY
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the user who created the row.

columns.Name: CREATION_DATE
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the date and time of the creation of the row.

columns.Name: LAST_UPDATED_BY
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the user who last updated the row.

columns.Name: LAST_UPDATE_DATE
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the date and time of the last update of the row.

columns.Name: LAST_UPDATE_LOGIN
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the session login associated to the user who last updated the row.

columns.Name: SEED_DATA_SOURCE
columns.Comments: Source of seed data record. A value of 'BULK_SEED_DATA_SCRIPT' indicates that record was bulk loaded. Otherwise, specifies the name of the seed data file.

columns.Name: TEMPLATE_BASED_SECTION_FLAG
columns.Comments: Flag to indicate whether section is based on template or not

columns.Name: LINKEDIN_ENABLED_FLAG
columns.Comments: Linkedin enable flag is use to indicate whether the section is supporting linkedin data ('Y') or not (null/ 'N')

columns.Name: SKILL_RECOMMEND_ENABLED_FLAG
columns.Comments: Flag to indicate whether Skills content section supports system-generated recommendations ('Y') or not (null/ 'N')

columns.Name: ORA_SEED_SET1
columns.Comments: Oracle internal use only. Indicates the edition-based redefinition (EBR) context of the row for SET1. Context values are Y or N.

columns.Name: ORA_SEED_SET2
columns.Comments: Oracle internal use only. Indicates the edition-based redefinition (EBR) context of the row for SET2. Context values are Y or N.
